summary: Implemented BaseRepository<T> generic CRUD class and four concrete repositories (TrainingPlan, TrainingDay, Exercise, PlanExercise) with full integration tests using sql.js in-memory SQLite. Created DatabaseAdapter interface to abstract expo-sqlite from test backends.
keyDecisions: Created DatabaseAdapter interface to abstract expo-sqlite sync API, enabling sql.js-based integration testing without native dependencies
Used factory functions (createXxxRepo) instead of classes for simpler DI and testability
bigint biz_key values are converted to number via toDbValue() at the SQLite boundary since both expo-sqlite and sql.js store INTEGER as JS number
BaseRepository.create() excludes 'id' column from INSERT (auto-increment), includes it in SELECT
Exercise soft-delete via is_deleted=1 flag; findAllActive() and findByCategory() filter is_deleted=0
TrainingPlan.activatePlan() deactivates all other plans first (single active plan constraint)
PlanExercise stores sets_config as JSON string; parse on read, stringify on write (application responsibility)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: src/db/repositories/base.repository.ts implements BaseRepository<T> interface (findById, findByBizKey, findAll, create, update, deleteById)
training-plan.repo.ts implements full CRUD for TrainingPlan with is_active toggle logic
training-day.repo.ts implements CRUD with findByPlanBizKey for querying days of a plan
exercise.repo.ts implements CRUD with soft-delete (is_deleted flag), findByCategory
plan-exercise.repo.ts implements CRUD with findByTrainingDayBizKey, JSON parse/serialize for sets_config
All repositories use snowflake biz_key for entity association (not auto-increment id)
Integration tests with in-memory SQLite pass for all CRUD operations

notes: Added sql.js and @types/sql.js as devDependencies for in-memory SQLite testing (better-sqlite3 requires native compilation). Installed via npm --legacy-peer-deps.
